subject: Computer Networking
title: GraphQL: Query What You Need
description: A quick, practical guide to GraphQL: Query What You Need.

hook.text: A REST API returns 50 fields. You only need 3. GraphQL lets you ask for exactly what you want.

buildup.text: GraphQL defines a schema. Clients write queries specifying the exact shape of data they need returned.

discovery.text: One endpoint serves all queries. No more juggling dozens of REST URLs for different data combinations.

twist.text: GraphQL shifts complexity to the server. Poorly written queries can accidentally request massive data joins.

climax.text: Facebook created GraphQL in 2012. GitHub, Shopify, and Yelp all offer public GraphQL APIs today.

punchline.text: GraphQL asks only for what it needs. No more, no less.

quiz.question: What advantage does GraphQL have over REST?
options: Clients request only the exact data fields they need
It uses binary encoding for faster transfers
It works without an internet connection
